course_objectives: To study the use of transfer function model for the analysis of physical systems and to introduce the components of control system.
To provide adequate knowledge in the time response of systems and steady state error analysis along with the understanding of closed-loop and open-loop system analysis in frequency domain.
To introduce the design of controllers and compensators for the stability analysis.
To introduce state variable representation of physical systems and study the stability analysis in state space approach.
course_outcomes: Differentiate between open-loop and closed-loop control systems and obtain the transfer function from the mathematical modeling of physical systems.
Determine transient and steady state responses of the system with first and second order and also to analyze its error coefficients.
Characterize the system stability using R-H criteria and root locus techniques.
Analyze the frequency domain response of the control systems.
Design the controllers and compensators to estimate the system stability.
Analyze the system in state space model through the concept of controllability and observability.

topics: Basic components of a control system
Open-loop control system and closed-loop control system
Examples of control system (air conditioner, cruise control, phase-locked loop, etc.)
Effects of feedback on overall gain
Difference and differential equations for LTI SISO and MIMO systems
Mathematical modeling of electrical and mechanical systems
Equivalence between the elements of different types of systems
Open-loop transfer function and closed-loop transfer function
Signal flow graph using Mason's gain formula.
Transient response and steady state responses
Response of first order and second order systems
Poles, Zeros, Order and Type of systems
Frequency response Performance specifications in the frequency domain
Cascade compensation in time domain and frequency domain
Design of lag, lead, lag-lead series compensators.
Dynamic system modeling in state space representation: Diagonal canonical form, Jordan canonical form
Solutions of state equations of LTI system
Conversion from state space model to transfer function model and vice versa
Stability analysis in state spaces: Concept of eigenvalues and eigenvectors
State transition matrix using Cayley-Hamilton theorem

textbooks: Norman S. Nise, Control Systems Engineering, 2019, 8th Edition, John Wiley & Sons, New Jersey, USA
reference_books: Farid Golnaraghi and Benjamin C. Kuo, Automatic Control Systems, 2017, 10th Edition, McGraw-Hill Education, India.
I.J. Nagarth and M. Gopal, Control Systems Engineering, 2018, 6th Edition, New Age International Pvt. Ltd., New Delhi, India.
Gene Franklin, J. Powell and Abbas Emami-Naeini, Feedback Control of Dynamic Systems, 2019, 8th Edition, Pearson Education, New Delhi, India.
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

course_objectives: To understand the transmitter and receiver blocks of various waveform coding techniques.
To analyze various line coding techniques in time and frequency domains.
To identify the role of baseband, bandpass formats and information theory for effective transmission of signals, combat ISI and to increase the reliability of transmission.
To understand the principles and importance of spread spectrum and multiple access in the context of communication.
course_outcomes: Comprehend the sampling and quantization process to recover the original signal.
Analyse the performance of various waveform and Line coding techniques.
Design the various baseband pulses for ISI free transmission over finite bandwidth channels.
Examine the BER and bandwidth efficiency of the Bandpass modulation techniques.
Analyse the digital communication system with spread spectrum modulation.
Infer the elements of information theory.

topics: Block diagram of a digital communication system
Reconstruction of a message from its samples
Practical aspects of sampling and signal recovery.
Pulse Code Modulation (PCM) - Uniform quantization, Quantization noise, Signal-to-Noise Ratio, Robust quantization.
Delta Modulation (DM) - Quantization noise in DM, Adaptive Delta Modulation.
Unipolar, Polar, Bipolar using NRZ and RZ, Manchester
Properties and applications of line codes
Power spectral density of line codes.
Baseband data transmission of binary data
correlative coding (duo binary and modified duo binary coding)
Coherent modulation techniques - BASK, BPSK, BFSK, QPSK, MSK, Higher-order PSK and QAM, BER and Bandwidth efficiency analysis.
Non-coherent modulation techniques - BASK, BFSK, DPSK.

modules.module_title: Spread Spectrum and Multiple Access Techniques
topics: Generation of PN sequence and its properties
Multiple access techniques: TDMA, FDMA, CDMA, SDMA.
Entropy, Mutual information and channel capacity theorem.
Fundamentals of error correction - Hamming codes.

textbooks: Simon Haykin, Digital Communications, 2017, 1st Edition, John Wiley, India.
reference_books: John G. Proakis, Masoud Salehi, Digital Communication, 2018, 5th Edition (Indian edition), Mc Graw Hill Education, India.
Bernard Sklar and Fredric J. Harris, Digital Communications: Fundamentals and Applications, 2020, 3rd Edition, Pearson, UK.
BP Lathi, Zhi Ding, Modern Digital And Analog Communication Systems, 2017, 4th Edition, Oxford university Press, India.
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

lab_component.mode_of_assessment: Continuous Assessment and Final Assessment Test

experiments.description: Generation and reconstruction of PCM, DPCM and DM
Generation of baseband signals using various line coding formats for the given binary sequence
Generation and detection of bandpass modulation techniques
BER analysis of bandpass modulation techniques
Generation of PN sequence and verification of its properties
Implementation of multiple access schemes

course_objectives: To summarize the usefulness of branching and looping statements in one dimension and multi-dimensional array programming.
To equip students with dynamic memory management through an expertise on pointers.
To introduce students the importance of polymorphism and inheritance in an object oriented programming.
To teach students the way of supervising exceptions through exception handlers and files through file handlers.
course_outcomes: Implement branching and looping statements to handle 1D and 2D arrays.
Realize the importance of pointers to manage the memory dynamically.
Comprehend the use of structures and unions to encapsulate different data types in programming.
Apply polymorphism and inheritance which are imbibed in object oriented programming.
Infer and handle different exceptions.
Access files in terms reading and writing through various file handlers.
Comprehend various elements of object-oriented programing paradigm and propose solutions through inheritance and polymorphism.

modules.module_title: C Programming Fundamentals, Arrays and Strings
topics: Variables: Reserved words, Data Types, Operators, Operator Precedence
Branching and Looping: if, if-else, nested if, if-else ladder, switch statement, goto statement
Loops: for, while and do...while, break and continue statements.
Arrays: One Dimensional array, Two-Dimensional Array
User Defined Functions: Declaration, Definition, call by value and call by reference
Storage Classes - Scope, Visibility and Lifetime of Variables.
Declaration and Access of Pointer Variables, Pointer arithmetic
Declaration, Initialization, Access of Structure Variables
Static Data Members, Static Member Functions and Objects
Inheritance Types of Inheritance: Single inheritance, Multiple Inheritance, Multi-level Inheritance, Hierarchical Inheritance

textbooks: Herbert Schildt, C: The Complete Reference, 2017, 4th Edition, McGraw Hill Education.
Herbert Schildt, C++: The Complete Reference, 2017, 4th Edition, McGraw Hill Education.
reference_books: Yashavant Kanetkar, Let Us C: 2020, 17th Edition, BPB Publications, 2020.
Stanley Lippman and Josee Lajoie, C++ Primer, 2012, 5th Edition, Addison-Wesley publishers
Byron S Gottfried, Programming with C, 2018, 2018, 4th Edition, Schaum's outline series.
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

lab_component.mode_of_assessment: Continuous Assessment and Final Assessment Test

experiments.description: Programs using basic control structures, branching and looping
Experiment the use of 1-D, 2-D arrays and strings and Functions
Demonstrate the application of pointers
Experiment structures and unions
Programs on basic Object-Oriented Programming constructs.
Demonstrate various categories of inheritance
Program to apply kinds of polymorphism.
Develop generic templates and Standard Template Libraries.
Demonstrate the use of Exception handling.
Demonstrate the working of file handling.

course_objectives: To get acquainted with different types of intelligent agents.
To understand the importance and significance of Machine learning.
To preface the essentials of Deep Learning.
course_outcomes: Comprehend different intelligent agents and its variants.
Solve the real-world problem using the various search algorithms.
Infuse various symbolic knowledge representation.
Employ intelligent agents for decision making.
Handle real-time issues using various learning methodologies.
Apply deep learning algorithms for solving real-world problems.

topics: Overview: Data Cleaning, Integration, Transformation & Reduction.
Statistical Methods: Naïve-Bayes, Nearest Neighbor, Decision Trees - Random Forest
Case studies - Machine Learning in Signal Processing, Intelligent Antenna.

textbooks: Stuart J Russell, Peter Norwig, Artificial Intelligence - A modern approach, 2015, 3rd edition, Pearson, India.
reference_books: Vinod Chandra S.S, Anand Hareendran S., Artificial Intelligence: Principles and Applications, 2020, 2nd Edition, PHI Learning Pvt. Ltd., India.
Alpaydin ethem, Introduction to Machine Learning, 2019, 3rd edition, PHI Learning Pvt. Ltd., India.
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

course_objectives: To summarize and analyze the concepts of signals, systems in time and frequency domain with the corresponding transformations.
To inculcate the design concepts of analog, digital IIR, FIR filters.
To instill diverse structures for realizing digital filters.
To infuse the novice concepts of Multirate digital signal processing.
course_outcomes: Classify and analyse Signals & Systems along with their time and frequency domain transformations.
Simplify Fourier transform computations using swift algorithms.
Examine various analog filter design techniques and their digitization.
Design FIR and IIR digital filters.
Realize digital filters using various system interconnections.
Design and formulate Multirate systems.

modules.module_title: Discrete Signals, Systems and frequency analysis
topics: Review of Discrete-Time Signals & Systems and frequency analysis
Z- transform: ROC, stability / causality analysis

modules.module_title: Discrete Fourier Transform, Properties and its applications
topics: Frequency analysis of signals using DFT
FFT Algorithm - Radix-2 FFT, Sparse FFT
Design techniques for analog filter - Butterworth and Chebyshev approximations
Constant group delay and zero phase filters.
IIR filter design: Bilinear transformation, Impulse Invariance
FIR Filter Design: Design characteristics of FIR filters with linear-phase
Frequency response of linear phase FIR filters
Design of FIR filters using windowing techniques: Rectangular, Bartlett, Hamming, Hanning, Blackmann, Kaiser
Direct, Cascade, Parallel, Lattice and Lattice Ladder Structures: All pass filter
Parallel all pass realization of IIR systems.
Introduction-Implementation of Sampling Rate Conversion: Polyphase Filter Structures
Interchange of Filters and Downsamplers / Upsamplers
Polyphase Structures for Decimation and Interpolation Filters
Structures for Rational Sampling Rate Conversion.

textbooks: John G. Proakis, Dimitris G Manolakis, Digital Signal Processing: Principles, Algorithms and Applications, 2022, 5th Edition, Pearson, USA
reference_books: A textbook of Digital Signal Processing, R.S.Kaler, M.Kulkarni, Umesh Gupta, 1st edition, 2019, Dream tech Press, Wiley, India
James McClellan, Ronal Schaeffer, Mark Yoder, Digital Signal Processing first, 2016, 2nd edition, Pearson, USA
Lizhe Tan, Jean Jiang, Digital Signal Processing: Fundamentals and applications, 3rd edition, 2018, Academic Press, USA
S.K.Mitra, Digital Signal Processing, 2013, 4th edition, TMH, New Delhi, India
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

lab_component.mode_of_assessment: Continuous Assessment and Final Assessment Test

experiments.description: Introduction to TMS320C6748 processor and code composer studio IDE.
Generation of elementary signals and illustration of simple signal processing operations on TMS320C6748 processor
Sampling and Reconstruction of CT signals, DTFT analysis
Biomedical / Speech / Audio Signal Analysis
Computational analysis using FFT
Design of IIR filter
Design of FIR filter using windowing techniques

course_objectives: To impart knowledge on AWS cloud management tools and services for developing cost-effective and reliable cloud solutions for real-world scenarios.
course_outcomes: Comprehend the AWS cloud concepts and service models.
Comprehend and differentiate between various AWS core, database services and its applications.
Apply appropriate security and compliance measures for cloud applications.
Design scalable, fault-tolerant and cost-effective AWS solutions using core services.
Apply AWS management tools, monitoring services to automate and troubleshoot tasks.
Configure and manage AWS networking and content delivery services.

topics: Cloud Service Models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Cloud Deployment Models: Public, Private, Hybrid
AWS Shared Responsibility Model in Security
Design Principles for AWS Cloud Architectures

textbooks: Saurabh Shrivastava, Neelanjali Srivastav, Alberto Artasanchez, 'AWS for Solutions Architects', 2nd edition, Packt Publishing, 2023.
Ashish Prajapati, Juan Carlos Ruiz, Marco Tamassia, 'AWS Cloud Computing Concepts and Tech Analogies', Packt Publishing, 2023.
reference_books: Logan Song, 'The Self-Taught Cloud Computing Engineer', Packt Publishing, 2023

course_objectives: To introduce and discuss the mechanism for antenna parameters, radiating principles, fundamental characteristics and design concepts of HF, UHF, Microwave antennas and arrays.
To design and analyse various passive and active microwave circuits.
To familiarize the operational principles of microwave sources and to characterize microwave networks.
course_outcomes: Examine the radiation mechanism of electromagnetic fields and identify the various antenna parameters.
Apply the design criteria to Linear, HF, UHF, microwave antenna and arrays.
Comprehend the performance of different microwave sources and ferrite devices.
Design and analyze the passive components at microwave frequencies.
Design and analyze the various passive circuits at microwave frequencies.
Infer the importance of high frequency transistors to design microwave amplifiers.

topics: Radiation mechanism - single wire, two wire and current distribution, Hertzian dipole, Dipole and monopole

Radiation pattern, beam width, field regions, radiation power density, radiation intensity, directivity and gain, bandwidth, polarization, input impedance, efficiency, antenna effective length and area, antenna temperature.
Friis transmission equation, Radar range equation.
Two element array, N-element linear array - broadside array, End fire array
Arrays: Planar array, circular array, Phased Array antenna (Qualitative study).
Wire Antennas: long wire, loop antenna, helical antenna.
Frequency independent antennas - spiral and log periodic antenna
Aperture antennas - Horn antenna, Parabolic reflector antenna
Microwave Tubes: TWT, Klystron amplifier, Reflex Klystron & Magnetron.
Semiconductor Devices: Gunn diode, Tunnel diode, IMPATT, TRAPATT, BARITT diodes, PIN Diode.
Microwave Networks: ABCD, 'S' parameter and its properties.
E-Plane Tee, H-Plane Tee, Magic Tee and Multi-hole directional coupler.
Principle of Faraday rotation, isolator, circulator and phase shifter.
T junction and resistive power divider, Wilkinson power divider, branch line coupler (equal & unequal), Rat Race Coupler
Filter design: Low pass filter (Butterworth and Chebyshev) - Richards transformation and stepped impedance methods.
Microwave amplifiers: Two port power gains, stability of the amplifier

textbooks: C.A. Balanis, Antenna Theory - Analysis and Design, 2016, 4th Edition, Wiley & Sons, New York, USA.
D. M. Pozar, Microwave engineering, 2013, 4th Edition, Wiley & Sons, USA.
reference_books: R Ludwig, Gene Bogdanov, RF Circuit design: Theory and applications, 2013, 2nd Edition, Pearson India.
John D Krauss, Antennas for all Applications, 2008, 4th Edition, Tata McGraw Hill, India.
courses.mode_of_evaluation: Continuous Assessment Test, Digital Assignment, Quiz and Final Assessment Test

lab_component.mode_of_assessment: Continuous Assessment and Final Assessment Test

experiments.description: Measurement of antenna input impedance
Measurement of antenna radiation pattern
Measurement of S-parameters for E-plane, H-plane and Magic Tee
Measurement of S-parameters for Directional Coupler
Measurement of S-parameters for Isolator and Circulator
Measurement of S-parameters of MIC devices
Design of Wilkinson power divider
Design of branch line and Rat race coupler
Design of low pass filters: Richards and Stepped impedance method
Design of matching circuits using quarter wave & single stub.
Design of dipole antenna
Design of Rectangular patch antenna
